Title: Identification of a key peptide cyclase for novel cyclic peptide discovery in Pseudostellaria heterophylla

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the necessary linear precursor substrate for heterophyllin B biosynthesis; co-expression with PhPCY3 yields HB
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Potential memory-improving and antidiabetic bioactivity noted for P. heterophylla orbitides/HB in paper context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected among precursor peptide genes mined from transcriptome; precursor transcript reduced in PhPCY3-silenced leaves
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supports HB production when transiently expressed in N. benthamiana with PhPCY3
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous co-expression with PhPCY3 in N. benthamiana; LC-MS; VIGS-associated expression/metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPreHB encodes the precursor substrate for HB. 2. Co-expression with PhPCY3 in N. benthamiana produced HB. 3. PhPreHB transcript abundance decreased in PhPCY3-silenced leaves alongside reduced HB accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Precursor peptides contain conserved leader and follower regions with diverse core peptide region; short CDSs were mined from transcriptomes.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable precursor module for reconstituting HB biosynthesis and orbitide discovery platforms
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in P. heterophylla

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor substrate for pseudostellarin E biosynthesis; co-expression with PhPCY3 yields PE
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supports PE production when co-expressed with PhPCY3 in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous co-expression with PhPCY3; LC-MS; VIGS-associated expression/metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPrePE serves as precursor for PE. 2. PhPCY3 cyclized PrePE to PE. 3. PhPrePE transcript and PE content decreased in PhPCY3-silenced tissues.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in P. heterophylla
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor for synthetic production of PE

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor substrate for pseudostellarin F biosynthesis; PhPCY3 cyclizes PrePF to PF
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supports PF production when co-expressed with PhPCY3 in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous co-expression with PhPCY3; LC-MS
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPrePF is a functional precursor gene. 2. PhPCY3 cyclized PrePF to produce PF in the transient expression system.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor for synthetic production of PF

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides precursor substrate for pseudostellarin G biosynthesis; co-expression with PhPCY3 yields PG
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Supports PG production when co-expressed with PhPCY3 in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient heterologous co-expression with PhPCY3; LC-MS; VIGS-associated expression/metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPrePG serves as precursor for PG. 2. PhPCY3 cyclized PrePG to PG. 3. PhPrePG transcript and PG content decreased in PhPCY3-silenced tissues.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector; promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in P. heterophylla
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor for synthetic production of PG

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: S9 serine protease; peptide cyclase 1-like; prolyl oligopeptidase domain (PF00326)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Macrocyclization/cyclization step of Caryophyllaceae-type cyclic peptide (orbitide) biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Linear precursor peptides/preprocessed precursor segments including HB [14-35] and precursor peptides for PE, PF, PG; requires C-terminal follower peptide
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Heterophyllin B; pseudostellarin E; pseudostellarin F; pseudostellarin G; byproduct HB [22-35] linear follower peptide
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Excises the C-terminal follower region from linear orbitide precursor peptides and cyclizes the core peptide into head-to-tail cyclic orbitides
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Heterophyllin B; pseudostellarin E; pseudostellarin F; pseudostellarin G; novel orbitides cyclo-[LDGPPPYF], cyclo-[WGSSTPHT], cyclo-[GLPIGAPWG], cyclo-[FGDVGPVI]
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reported in paper/background as having potential for improving memory and treating diabetes; orbitides also noted as having anticancer/antitumor potential
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes in planta orbitide accumulation and is essential for orbitide cyclization; silencing decreases HB, PE, and PG; heterologous expression enables biosynthesis of known and novel orbitides
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In transiently infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, supports orbitide production upon co-expression with precursor genes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key tailoring enzyme and primary enzyme for orbitide biosynthesis; dominant cyclization step
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tissue context (root-enriched accumulation/expression); transient Agrobacterium-mediated heterologous expression in N. benthamiana; VIGS in P. heterophylla
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assay with purified MBP-tagged protein; VIGS silencing in P. heterophylla; LC-MS metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PhPCY3 showed strong positive correlation with orbitide accumulation and root-biased expression. 2. Co-expression with precursor genes in N. benthamiana was sufficient to produce HB and other known orbitides without PhOLPs. 3. Purified PhPCY3 converted HB [14-35] into cyclic HB and released HB [22-35] follower peptide, but could not cyclize the short HB [14-21] substrate directly. 4. VIGS knockdown of PhPCY3 significantly reduced HB, PE, and PG in P. heterophylla. 5. PhPCY3 enabled biosynthesis of four novel orbitides from mined precursor genes. 6. N500 and S502 were identified as critical residues for full cyclase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PhPCY3 is described as the second validated orbitide cyclase after SvPCY1. Conserved catalytic triad residues S571/D662/H704 are present, but unique N500 and S502 in the prolyl oligopeptidase domain distinguish functionality from non-functional homologs. Cannot directly convert the short core-only substrate HB [14-21], indicating dependence on follower peptide context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful key enzyme for synthetic biology production and discovery of known/novel orbitides; can be stacked with precursor peptide genes for metabolic engineering platforms in plants
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT transient expression vector in N. benthamiana; promoter not specified
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NCBI SRA SUB14839290; transcriptome and genome mining
